healthy.label: Healthy cassava
steps: Ensure proper spacing to allow airflow.
healthy.consult: If symptoms appear, consult your extension worker before taking action.

cassava_mosaic.label: Cassava Mosaic Disease (CMD)
cassava_mosaic.opening: This looks like Cassava Mosaic Disease.
steps: Remove and destroy heavily infected plants — do not compost them.
Use only clean, certified cuttings from disease-free plants for your next planting.
Control whitefly populations, which spread this disease.
Plant CMD-resistant varieties if available in your area.
cassava_mosaic.consult: Consult your extension worker before applying any chemical treatment.

cassava_brown_streak.label: Cassava Brown Streak Disease (CBSD)
cassava_brown_streak.opening: This may be Cassava Brown Streak Disease.
steps: Do not use stems from this plant as cuttings.
Report this to your extension worker — CBSD is a priority disease for surveillance.
Remove infected plants carefully and bury or burn them away from the field.
cassava_brown_streak.consult: This disease requires expert confirmation. Please consult your extension worker immediately.

pest_damage.label: Pest damage (Green Mite or Mealybug)
pest_damage.opening: Your plant shows signs of pest damage.
steps: Inspect the underside of leaves for mites or mealybugs.
Remove heavily infested leaves and destroy them.
Encourage natural predators by avoiding broad-spectrum insecticides.
pest_damage.consult: Consult your extension worker before applying any pesticide.

unknown.label: Unclear — expert review needed
unknown.opening: I am not confident about what this is.
steps: Take several more photos from different angles and in good lighting.
Show the photos to your nearest extension worker or agronomist.
unknown.consult: Do not apply any treatment until an expert has confirmed the problem.
